title: aureolaria pectinata
intro: Aureolaria pectinata, also known as the rosy plectritis or sticky monkey flower, is a perennial plant species in the bumblebee balm family that is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Aureolaria pectinata include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Aureolaria pectinata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
